extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates proceedings by direction and category: I counted only the “Against” rows, namely criminal proceedings of 0 for the Company, 1 for subsidiaries, 3 for Directors, 1 for Promoters and 1 for KMP/SMP, excluding all “By” rows; statutory/regulatory actions were 0, 4 and 1 for the Company, subsidiaries and Directors respectively, while tax proceedings were 4 against the Company and 23 against subsidiaries. For the aggregate amount against the Company and subsidiaries, I used the table’s stated amounts of ₹1,391.41 million and ₹403.70 million, respectively, totaling ₹1,795.11 million, or ₹179.511 crore; promoter/director/KMP amounts and all “By” rows were excluded. The contingent-liabilities disclosure is as of June 30, 2025 and states ₹32.86 million for claims against the Company not acknowledged as debts, equivalent to ₹3.286 crore; no explicit total row is shown, so this disclosed quantified contingent amount is used. The only concentration percentages disclosed relate to products or subsidiaries, not company-wide top-five or top-ten customers; therefore customer concentration is not extracted. Monetary tables use ₹ million, converted to crore by dividing by 10 (₹1,795.11 million = ₹179.511 crore; ₹32.86 million = ₹3.286 crore).
